regions.text: The first living things were eating, growing organisms which divided to become others like them. Under a microscope, a cell looks like a bit of clear jelly with a thin wall round it.
Very small openings in the walls of the cell let food in and waste out. In every cell there is a nucleus, which organizes the work of the cell.
The nucleus is the seat of government. It is to the cell what London is to Britain. jelly: soft material. nucleus: cell center. organize: give every part its work.
FUTURE: will organize. PRESENT: organize(s). PAST: organized.
